data_collected: Custom developer portfolio details (name, geographic location, personal website link)
Request metadata (temporarily stored for debugging and DDoS prevention)
security_practices: Restricted staff access to databases (technical support only)
No volunteer access to private data
Daily emergency backups deleted after a few days
GDPR compliance extended to all users globally
Immediate deletion of data from active servers upon user request
user_rights: Right to download all data in JSON format
Right to set profile data to private
Right to delete specific data or the entire account
Right to access, rectify, and port data
Right to object to automated decision-making
Right to opt-out of Google Analytics
red_flags: Developer portfolio is public by default
Data is shared with Google/GitHub if social login is used
justification: The policy is exceptionally transparent and user-friendly. They explicitly state they do not sell data, offer a clear data portability option (JSON), and apply high GDPR standards to all users. The score is slightly elevated only due to the presence of Google Ads and the fact that profiles are public by default.
